title: Tc2000 Scanner Pricing Guide 2026: All Plans, Costs & Hidden Fees
meta_title: TC2000 Scanner Pricing Guide 2026: Plans, Costs & Fees
meta_description: TC2000 Scanner starts at $24.99/mo but real-time data adds $14.99–$34.97/mo. Full 2026 pricing breakdown: every plan, hidden costs, and who it's worth it for.
excerpt: TC2000 Gold costs $24.99/mo — but real-time data adds up to $35/mo more. Here's every plan, actual all-in cost, and who should pay for it in 2026.
content:
TC2000 Pricing Plans in 2026: What Each Tier Actually Costs

TC2000 uses a tiered subscription model with a base platform fee plus optional real-time data add-ons. The headline price you'll see advertised — $24.99 per month — covers the Gold plan, which is where most active traders land. But that's rarely your all-in cost. Here's what every tier includes:

  • Free (Silver Basic): Charting and basic watchlists with 20-minute delayed data. No scanning. Useful for browsing the platform, not for trading.
  • Silver ($9.99/mo): Adds scanning capability with delayed data. Suitable for end-of-day traders who don't need live prices during market hours.
  • Gold ($24.99/mo): Full scanning access, Condition Wizard, EasyScan presets, 240+ technical indicators, 129+ fundamental variables, and options charting. This is the tier reviewed and rated 4.3/5 across trader communities.
  • Platinum ($39.99/mo): Adds multi-monitor layouts, advanced portfolio analytics, and priority support. Built for professional traders managing multiple strategies simultaneously.

The Gold tier at $24.99/mo is what most reviews reference, including our detailed TC2000 Scanner review. It covers the scanning engine in full — but the data feeding those scans is billed separately.

Real-Time Data Add-Ons: The Hidden Costs Nobody Warns You About

This is where TC2000 pricing gets complicated — and where traders consistently get surprised after signing up.

The base subscription delivers 20-minute delayed quotes. If you're scanning intraday for breakouts or momentum plays, delayed data is essentially useless. Real-time feeds are exchange-licensed add-ons billed monthly on top of your subscription:

  • NYSE + AMEX real-time: $14.99/month
  • NASDAQ real-time: $14.99/month
  • Full real-time bundle (all U.S. exchanges): $34.97/month
  • Options real-time data: Additional fee depending on exchanges selected

A Gold subscriber who wants complete real-time coverage is paying $24.99 + $34.97 = $59.96/month minimum. For active traders, that's the realistic baseline — not $24.99.

To be fair, this isn't unique to TC2000. Exchange fees are a regulatory cost passed through to end users across the industry. But competitors handle this differently — some bundle basic real-time data into their base price. Worth factoring into any comparison.

What the $24.99 Gold Plan Actually Delivers

Beyond the price, here's what TC2000 Gold gives you for that monthly fee:

Speed and Scanning Power

TC2000's scan engine is genuinely fast — processing thousands of stocks in seconds. For traders running daily pre-market scans or intraday setups, this matters. Slower screeners create bottlenecks exactly when you need answers quickest.

Condition Wizard

TC2000's standout feature for non-programmers. The Condition Wizard lets you build complex multi-condition scans through a visual interface — no formula writing, no coding. You can layer technical conditions (RSI crosses, moving average relationships, volume spikes) and fundamental filters (P/E ratios, earnings growth, revenue trends) in the same scan. Most screeners force you to choose one or the other.

240+ Technical Indicators + 129+ Fundamental Variables

The indicator library is deep enough for most systematic strategies. The real differentiator is combining both data types: you can screen for stocks meeting technical chart conditions and fundamental criteria simultaneously — something many screeners split across different tools.

EasyScan Templates

Pre-built scan templates for common setups — breakouts, pullbacks, trend followers, momentum plays. Useful starting points that experienced traders customize, and genuinely helpful for newer traders learning what to scan for.

Options Charting and Scanning

TC2000 includes historical options charting and the ability to scan options alongside stocks. This is included in Gold, not locked behind a higher tier — a meaningful inclusion given how many competing screeners treat options as an expensive upsell.

Quick Comparison: TC2000 vs. Competing Screeners in 2026

Here's how TC2000 stacks up against the platforms traders most commonly evaluate alongside it:

Platform Base Price Real-Time Data All-In Cost Rating Best For
TC2000 Gold $24.99/mo +$14.99–$34.97/mo ~$60/mo 4.3/5 US swing + options traders
Finviz Elite $39.50/mo Included $39.50/mo 4.5/5 Fast fundamental + technical screening
ChartMill $25.00/mo Included (15-min delay on free) $25.00/mo 4.2/5 Technical pattern detection
Benzinga Pro $47.00/mo Included $47.00/mo 4.1/5 News-driven traders, catalyst plays

When you account for real-time data, TC2000's effective price is competitive but no longer the budget option. See our TC2000 vs. Finviz comparison for a deeper breakdown of which screener wins at different use cases, and our TC2000 vs. ChartMill comparison if you're choosing between chart-focused platforms.

Who Should Actually Pay for TC2000

TC2000 earns its rating for specific types of traders. Here's where it genuinely excels — and where you're better off elsewhere:

TC2000 Is Worth It If You Are:

  • An active US stock trader who scans daily: The speed and Condition Wizard combination is hard to beat for building and running daily scanning routines across the full US market.
  • A swing trader who reviews charts visually: TC2000's charting interface is built for traders who want to scan for candidates and immediately review the chart — the workflow is seamless in a way that purely data-table screeners aren't.
  • An options trader wanting historical options data: The ability to chart and scan options alongside underlying stocks in a single platform saves meaningful time for traders running options strategies on individual stocks.
  • Someone who wants power without programming: Condition Wizard genuinely lowers the barrier to complex scanning. If you've been frustrated trying to write scan code in other platforms, TC2000's visual approach is a significant quality-of-life improvement.

TC2000 Is Probably Not Worth It If You Are:

  • Trading futures, forex, or crypto: TC2000 covers US stocks, ETFs, and options only. No exceptions. If your strategy touches any other asset class, you'll need a separate platform — making TC2000 redundant overhead.
  • Trading international markets: Same limitation. US markets only.
  • Budget-conscious and primarily doing end-of-day analysis: The Silver plan at $9.99/mo covers delayed scanning. But if you need real-time data anyway, the cost gap between Silver and Gold shrinks to the point where Gold makes more sense.
  • Primarily news-driven: TC2000 has no news integration worth noting. Traders who make decisions based on catalysts should look at Benzinga Pro vs. TC2000 — Benzinga Pro's news feed is a genuinely different product category.

TC2000 Limitations Worth Knowing Before You Subscribe

A 4.3/5 rating means TC2000 is genuinely good — it doesn't mean it's perfect. Two limitations stand out enough to affect the purchase decision:

US-Only Coverage Is a Hard Wall

This isn't a missing feature you can work around — it's a structural constraint. TC2000 licenses US exchange data and has no international market coverage. If you ever want to expand beyond US equities, you'll need to add a second platform. Factor that into your total cost of ownership.

Real-Time Data Is Not Optional for Most Traders

The marketing headline price assumes you're comfortable with 20-minute delay. Most active traders are not. The $14.99–$34.97 add-on range means your honest monthly budget planning should start from $40–$60, not $25. This isn't a dealbreaker — the all-in price is still competitive — but going in with the right number matters for your tool budget.

Learning Curve on Condition Wizard

Despite being built for non-programmers, Condition Wizard has a meaningful learning curve. Traders switching from simpler screeners often spend their first week just learning how to structure compound conditions correctly. Budget time for that, especially if you're building custom scans from scratch rather than modifying EasyScan templates.

Final Verdict: Is TC2000 Worth the Price in 2026?

At $24.99/month base with a realistic all-in cost of $55–$60/month including real-time data, TC2000 Gold is a strong choice for its target user — but only if that user fits the profile.

The value proposition holds clearly for: US-focused traders who run daily scans, chart-driven swing traders, and options traders who want one platform to cover both the stock and its derivatives. For those traders, TC2000's Condition Wizard, chart-scan integration, and scanning speed justify the price.

The value proposition breaks down for: traders working in any market outside US equities, traders who primarily trade on news catalysts, and traders for whom the real-time add-on cost pushes total spend past their tool budget ceiling.

If you're still evaluating whether TC2000 fits your workflow, read the full TC2000 Scanner review before committing to a paid tier — the free plan is available and gives you enough exposure to evaluate the interface before your first charge. The Condition Wizard is either going to click for you quickly or feel unnecessarily complex. Knowing which camp you're in before month two is worth the trial period.

Bottom line: TC2000 earns its 4.3/5 rating for the traders it's designed for. It doesn't try to be everything to everyone — and that focus is a feature, not a limitation, as long as your trading aligns with what it covers.
